fields.description:
## Why

The task-decomposition SOTA report (epic 6982, §5 of docs/research/task-decomposition-sota-2026.md;
prior art T1: CodePlan's layer-cake antipattern, claude-task-master's DAG ordering, Kiro's
"waves") identifies two recurring **decomposition-SHAPE smells** in CONTAINER plans (a parent
split into children) that the existing container criteria G3 (child-coverage) and G4
(child-consistency) do NOT target:

- **layer-cake split** — children partitioned by ARCHITECTURAL LAYER (a "DB/schema" child, a
  "service/API" child, a "frontend/UI" child) instead of vertical, independently-shippable
  slices. Each child is un-demoable alone and the integration risk is deferred to the end.
  G3 (does the union cover the parent's ACs?) and G4 (7 cross-child interaction modes) both pass
  a layer-cake — coverage is complete and the layers are individually consistent; the DEFECT is
  the partition axis itself.
- **consumed-artifact-without-ordering-edge** — a child NAMES/CONSUMES an artifact a SIBLING
  produces (its output file, schema, event, symbol), yet there is NO ordering dependency
  (depends_on/blocks) between them, so a parallel-agent scheduler can start the consumer before
  the producer exists. G4's "dependency gap"/"ordering violation" modes are broad and
  benign-filtered; this is the specific, high-signal producer→consumer-without-edge shape.

Both are SHAPE properties of the (parent, children) decomposition — not coverage, not code
grounding. This story ships one focused advisory container criterion for exactly that surface.

## What

Add one new **advisory, AGENT-tier, CONTAINER** plan-review criterion, id `decomp-shape`
(facet `container`, like G3/G4 — NOT codebase-grounding), that audits the (parent, children,
sibling-roster) decomposition for the two smells above. It runs on the EXISTING container
fan-out — no new pass, no new engine construct:

- **Mechanism seam (cited).** Container criteria are pulled out of the normal agent path and run
  via the dedicated per-child loop: `CONTAINER_CRITERIA = ("G3", "G4")` at
  `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/pass1.py:43` (partitioned at pass1.py:373-374), dispatched by
  `passes.pass1_container(...)` at `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/passes.py:357` — which builds the
  combined rubric from the `criteria` list (`rubric = "\n\n".join(_criterion_block(c) ...)`,
  passes.py:387), validates model self-reported attribution against `valid_ids`
  (passes.py:381, 428), and hands the finder parent + whole children + the complete sibling
  roster. Adding `decomp-shape` to `CONTAINER_CRITERIA` fans it out through that same seam with
  per-criterion + per-child attribution; nothing else changes.
- **Registration seam.** A packaged built-in LLM criterion must be added to
  `registry.CANONICAL_LLM` and `registry.AGENT_TIER` (like G3/G4; NOT `CODEBASE_GROUNDED` — its
  facet is `container`, not codebase-grounding), be given a routing entry in
  `criteria_routing.json` (`exec:AGENT`, `facet:container`, `applies_at.scope:["container"]`,
  `default_posture:"advisory"`, `block_threshold:0.95`), a rubric prompt at
  `reviewers/plan_review_decomp_shape.md` (front-matter `title`→guide name, body→scenario), and a
  regenerated `## decomp-shape` guide section — else `registry.validate_packaged_routing()` flags
  ORPHAN and CI fails (the bit that hit R2/b080), and `validate_criteria_guide()` flags the
  missing section.
- **Two criterion-local checklist sub-answers** (gated-then-detected): `has_container_decomposition`
  (GATE — parent has >=2 children forming a decomposition; `no` -> not-applicable -> PASS) and
  `decomposition_shape_sound` (`no` when a layer-cake partition OR a
  consumed-artifact-without-ordering-edge is present — the finding).
- **Bounded sanity harness.** Container criteria are NOT runnable over the inline-text eval path
  today (`eval_solver._run_criterion_case` raises for `cid in CONTAINER_CRITERIA`,
  eval_solver.py:105). This story extends that arm to run a container criterion via
  `pass1_container` when the fixture carries a `children:` list — a small, contained enhancement
  that makes `decomp-shape` (and G3/G4) calibratable through `rebar criteria eval`, keeping the
  sanity run bounded to one live container call per fixture.

It ships **advisory posture (never blocks)** — its permanent posture per this epic's operator
direction. Promotion to blocking is a **future dogfood-gated `criteria_routing.json` change**,
DOUBLE-gated: (1) the standing per-criterion effectiveness recorder (`criterion_effectiveness.py`)
must show it earns its blocks (high `detection_proxy`, low `blocking_fp_proxy`, sufficient
sample) AND (2) E6's judge order-stability must improve above floor — E6's order-shuffle
reliability FAILED (fleiss_kappa=0.55 < 0.6 floor, raw=0.7 < 0.8; e6_summary.json), which is a
declared R3 PREREQUISITE FAILURE (e6_judge_reliability.py:672): an order-sensitive judge makes a
BLOCKING container verdict untrustworthy, so blocking-promotion is deferred until order-stability
clears floor. Advisory shipping is unaffected — a non-blocking coaching nudge is safe under
judge instability.

## Scope

IN: the criterion + its routing/registry/rubric/guide, the eval_solver container-arm extension,
the bounded sanity fixtures + committed result, deterministic unit tests, and the promotion-gate
documentation note. OUT: any blocking promotion (future dogfood-gated), the E2/E3 mutant corpus
(permanently rejected on cost; sanity uses hand-authored fixtures), dogfood-accumulation waits.

Files: `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/registry.py`, `.../criteria_routing.json`, `.../pass1.py`,
`src/rebar/llm/reviewers/plan_review_decomp_shape.md`, `src/rebar/llm/evals/eval_solver.py`,
`docs/plan-review-criteria-guide.md`,
`src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/plan-review-decomp-shape.eval.yaml`,
`docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/runs/decomp_shape_sanity.json`,
`tests/unit/test_decomp_shape_criterion.py`, `tests/unit/test_threshold_recalibration.py`,
`docs/plan-review-gate.md`.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `decomp-shape` is REGISTERED as an advisory, AGENT-tier CONTAINER criterion and is
  non-orphan. Proof: `python -c "from rebar.llm.plan_review import registry as r, json; assert
  'decomp-shape' in r.CANONICAL_LLM and 'decomp-shape' in r.AGENT_TIER and 'decomp-shape' not in
  r.CODEBASE_GROUNDED; assert r.validate_packaged_routing()==[]; import json;
  e=json.load(open('src/rebar/llm/plan_review/criteria_routing.json'))['decomp-shape']; assert
  e['exec']=='AGENT' and e['facet']=='container' and e['default_posture']=='advisory'; from
  rebar.llm.plan_review.pass1 import CONTAINER_CRITERIA; assert 'decomp-shape' in
  CONTAINER_CRITERIA; print('OK')"` prints OK, and `rebar explain decomp-shape` exits 0 with a
  `## decomp-shape` section (`validate_criteria_guide()==[]`).
- [ ] Deterministic (no-LLM) unit tests for the non-LLM logic pass — registration invariants,
  routing/finding shape (advisory, container facet, scope), prompt-contract front-matter +
  sub-answer keys, the eval_solver container-arm (a fake runner threads through `pass1_container`),
  the eval-fixture shape, and zero-wiring auto-inclusion into the effectiveness recorder. Proof:
  `pytest tests/unit/test_decomp_shape_criterion.py -q` exits 0.
- [ ] Bounded sanity check committed: hand-authored fixtures — one known layer-cake container
  plan + one known consumed-artifact-without-ordering-edge plan (must-fire), plus vertical-slice
  and well-ordered containers (must-not-fire) — the criterion FIRES on both positives and is
  CLEAN on the negatives. Proof: `docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/runs/decomp_shape_sanity.json`
  preserves the raw per-case `rebar criteria eval decomp-shape --output json` output and records
  recall over the positives (target 1.0) and false-accept over the negatives; any negative fire
  is ADVISORY (never blocks), recorded as a promotion-gate FP signal. <=8 live single-criterion
  container runs total; NO E2/E3 corpus, NO batch eval.
- [ ] The criterion auto-flows into the standing recorder. Proof: a unit assertion feeds one
  synthetic firing row through `criterion_effectiveness.firings_from_review` /
  `compute_effectiveness` and asserts `decomp-shape` is auto-included with ZERO per-criterion
  wiring (the id appears in the `--report` metrics map).
- [ ] The advisory->blocking promotion gate is documented, citing d8a5's protocol in
  `docs/plan-review-gate.md` §"Standing per-criterion effectiveness recorder + advisory->blocking
  promotion gate", and NOTING that E6's order-sensitivity (fleiss_kappa=0.55 < floor) defers
  blocking-promotion until order-stability clears floor (a second gate on top of dogfood
  effectiveness). Proof: the section names `decomp-shape` and the E6 order-stability prerequisite.
- [ ] [operator-attested] The change is landed on main: `LLM-Review +1` AND `Verified +1` and
  submitted (Gerrit), attested in-session.
